name: Fortify
text: Add 2 HP to a Ward you control. If you control no Ward, create one with 1 HP instead. Then remove 1 Burn marker from yourself.
comment: Good early-game Ward sustain. Rewards players who already established a Ward on a prior turn.

name: Arcane Shell
text: Create a Ward with 1 HP. If this Ward is destroyed this round, draw 2 cards.
comment: Lightweight Ward that cycles on destruction — encourages opponent to attack it and rewards the Abjuration player for taking hits.

name: Ward Pulse
text: Each Ward you control gains 1 HP. If you control a Ward, deal 1 damage.
comment: Scales with Ward density. Weak early but synergizes with multi-Ward strategies at higher levels.

name: Aegis
text: Until the start of your next turn, you cannot be targeted by spells that have only 1 component attached.
comment: Punishes opponents who haven't fully loaded their spells. Creates counterplay window pressure.

name: Stone Stance
text: Until the end of the round, the first Reaction you play costs 1 fewer S component (minimum 1).
comment: Enables early Reaction spam by discounting the first one. Strong opener for a reactive game plan.

name: Dampen
text: The next spell your opponent casts this turn deals 1 less damage.
comment: Simple but versatile. Pairs with other damage-prevention Reactions to stack reduction.

name: Echo Shield
text: Prevent 1 damage. If you have a Ward in play, prevent 2 damage instead.
comment: Rewards proactive Ward setup. A tempo puzzle for the opponent: destroy the Ward first or risk extra mitigation.

name: Grounding
text: Prevent 1 damage. Draw 1 card from your Component Deck.
comment: Rare card draw for Abjuration. Small upside turns a pure-prevention Reaction into a slight tempo gain.

name: Mana Drain
text: When your opponent attaches a component to a prepared spell, you may discard this card to immediately remove that component and return it to their hand.
comment: High disruption ceiling — delays a spell by a full turn. Telegraphed once face-up, so opponent can play around it.

name: Runic Seal
text: Place a Seal counter on target prepared spell. That spell cannot be cast this round.
comment: Strong soft-lock. Costs 2 S symbols at Level 1, which makes it a premium Level 1 opener.

name: Absorb
text: Prevent all damage from one spell that targets you. Gain HP equal to half the prevented damage, rounded down.
comment: Full prevention with a heal attached — turns the opponent's single biggest hit into HP for you. A strong reactive swing.

name: Reflective Ward
text: Create a Ward with 3 HP. When this Ward prevents damage, deal 1 damage to your opponent.
comment: Turns defense into slow chip damage. Synergizes with anything that buffs Ward HP (Fortify / Ward Pulse).

name: Interrupt
text: Remove all components from target prepared spell. Your opponent draws 1 card.
comment: Strips a nearly-ready spell back to zero. The draw softens the hit but opponent loses tempo and the surprise factor.

name: Phase Shift
text: Cancel target spell. You may immediately attach 1 component from your hand to a prepared spell.
comment: Combines cancellation with forward progression — reacts and sets up your own next cast simultaneously.

name: Counterbind
text: Cancel target spell that requires a Material (M) component.
comment: Tag-sensitive counter for Material (M) spells. Punishes the Divination school specifically; the off-color SM cost signals cross-school tech.

name: Break Form
text: Cancel target spell that requires a Somatic (S) component.
comment: Anti-Abjuration tool that is itself an Abjuration card — great in the mirror match. Forms the Somatic (S) half of the tag-based cancel suite alongside Counterbind (M).

name: Arcane Anchor
text: Until the start of your next turn, your opponent cannot play Reaction spells.
comment: Offensive play for a defensive deck — punch your own spells through without fear of a Reaction. High-value surprise.

name: Aetheric Lock
text: Until the end of the round, your opponent's Reaction spells cost 1 additional S component to play.
comment: Soft-taxes Reactions rather than banning them. Particularly brutal against opposing Abjuration mirrors.

name: Stonewarden
text: Create a Ward with 2 HP. While this Ward is active, you cannot be targeted by Level 1 spells.
comment: Provides blanket immunity to the entire early-game toolbox. Pressures opponent to commit higher-level spells to break through.

name: Sentinel Rune
text: Create a Ward with 3 HP. While active, the first time each round your opponent casts a spell, draw 1 card.
comment: Off-color cost (VS) signals it's a cross-school hybrid. Rewards sitting on the Ward — persistent card advantage as long as it lives.

name: Shatter Ward
text: Sacrifice one of your Wards. Negate all damage from target spell.
comment: Converts an expendable Ward into a full cancel. Great with low-HP Wards like Arcane Shell that are about to break anyway.

name: Aegis Eternal
text: Create a Ward with 6 HP. While active, reduce all damage dealt to you by 1 (minimum 0).
comment: Mid-level defensive anchor. The passive reduction stacks with Dampen and other prevention effects for near-immunity turns.

name: Total Negation
text: Cancel target spell. Your opponent cannot cast additional spells this turn.
comment: The heavyweight Reaction payoff. Costs a full round of S-component investment but ends an opponent's offensive turn entirely.

name: Inversion Field
text: Until the end of the round, each time a spell would deal damage to you, gain that HP instead (maximum 5 HP gained this way).
comment: Reverses an all-in offensive turn into a healing windfall. Costs SSM to balance — opponents who read it can pivot, making it a bluffing tool.

name: Sanctum
text: Until the end of the round, your opponent must target your Wards before targeting you directly. All your Wards gain 2 HP.
comment: Combines forced targeting with a Ward buff — either opponent grinds through buffed Wards or wastes their turn.

name: Abjure the Wicked
text: Cancel target spell. Deal 2 damage to your opponent for each component attached to the cancelled spell.
comment: Punishes fully loaded spells most — a 3-component VSM spell results in 6 damage on cancel. Off-color V cost signals aggression.

name: Ritual Ward
text: Create a Ward with 5 HP. When this Ward is destroyed, immediately create a replacement Ward with 2 HP.
comment: Double-life Ward. The replacement is weak enough to be cleared but forces the opponent to commit two removal actions.

name: Unraveling
text: Remove all components from ALL of your opponent's prepared spells. They draw 1 card for each spell affected.
comment: Nuclear disruption. Opponent draws cards, which softens the blow and prevents feel-bad moments, but they lose all setup simultaneously.

name: Fortress
text: Create a Ward with 4 HP. Wards you control cannot be targeted or destroyed by your opponent this round.
comment: Protects your Ward infrastructure from removal effects. Forces the opponent to grind through your Wards the hard way.

name: Penumbral Seal
text: Choose a prepared spell your opponent controls. Exile it face-down until the end of the round. At end of round, return it to prepared spells face-up.
comment: Temporarily removes a threat without destroying it — stalls their gameplan for a full round. Flavor: the spell is locked in magical shadow.

name: Ward Collapse
text: Destroy one of your Wards. Deal damage equal to that Ward's current HP to your opponent.
comment: The core damage finisher for a Ward-heavy game. Converts a large late-game Ward (Aegis Eternal, Ritual Ward, Ward Eternal) into a direct damage burst. Rewards players who built Ward HP up with Fortify and Ward Pulse earlier.

name: Reckoning
text: Deal damage to your opponent equal to the total amount of damage you have prevented this round.
comment: Rewards a patient defensive game — the more attacks you've weathered and mitigated, the harder this hits. Pairs naturally with Absorb and other prevention effects to set up a large burst in the same round.

name: Backlash
text: Deal 3 damage to your opponent for each Reaction spell you have played this round.
comment: Turns a reactive turn into an offensive payoff — a player who has played 3 Reactions this round deals 9 damage. The SSM cost mirrors Inversion Field, reading the M component as the 'calculation and timing' cost.

name: Exhausting Aura
text: Until the end of the round, each time your opponent plays a Reaction, they take 3 damage.
comment: Punishes a reactive opponent: every Reaction they play costs them 3 HP. Forces a dilemma — keep reacting and bleed out, or sit on their cards and let your spells through.

name: Banishing Bolt
text: Deal 5 damage to your opponent. If you have a Ward in play, deal 7 damage instead.
comment: Simple and readable. The Ward bonus is modest enough that it isn't mandatory but rewards having one up. A pure damage card with a defensive condition — feels like Abjuration without being mechanical about it.

name: Overcharge
text: Sacrifice all your Wards. Deal 2 damage to your opponent for each HP those Wards had combined.
comment: High-risk, high-reward. A player sitting on Aegis Eternal (6 HP), Ritual Ward (5 HP), and a Reflective Ward (3 HP) could deal 28 damage in one cast. The SSV cost signals this is a deliberate off-school spike — you're converting your entire defensive position into a kill shot.

name: Retributive Strike
text: When a spell deals damage to you, cancel it and deal double that spell's damage to your opponent instead.
comment: A damage-reflection Reaction that doesn't just mirror an incoming hit — it doubles it. The full SSS cost makes it compete with Total Negation for the same slot: a safe cancel versus a risky double-damage gamble.

name: Shieldbreaker Pulse
text: Deal 4 damage to your opponent. If they have a Ward in play, destroy it and deal 4 additional damage instead.
comment: Anti-Ward finisher for the mirror matchup. Removes the opponent's protection AND punishes them for having had it. Strong in any matchup where the opponent runs Wards, negligible bonus against Evocation.

name: Absolute Defense
text: Until the end of this round, you cannot be damaged by spells. Your opponent cannot play Reactions.
comment: Full immunity. Requires two SS dual-component cards to satisfy the 2-card cap. Reserved for Level 15+ late-game dominance.

name: Ward Eternal
text: Create a Ward with 10 HP. This Ward cannot be destroyed or targeted by your opponent's effects. When this Ward is destroyed by damage, you gain 5 HP.
comment: The ultimate Ward. Needs SSM + S or SS + SM component pairing. Aspirational card players build toward — rarely seen but devastating.

name: Archmage's Seal
text: Cancel all spells currently on the stack. Neither player may cast spells or play Reactions until the start of your next turn.
comment: Board-reset Reaction. Wipes the entire stack, not just one spell. For epic late-game stalebreaks. A true Archmage moment.

name: Warden's Wrath
text: Deal damage to your opponent equal to your current HP.
comment: Scales with survivability — the more damage you have absorbed and prevented over the course of the game

name: Final Reckoning
text: Deal 4 damage to your opponent for each round that has passed in this match.
comment: Late-game bomb that rewards survival over time. In a round 7-8 game this deals 28-32 damage — enough to close from nearly full HP. The SSSM cost mirrors Ward Eternal, putting both at the same premium. A player has to choose which Level 4 bomb to build their deck toward.

name: Null Burst
text: Cancel all spells currently being cast by your opponent. Deal 3 damage for each spell cancelled this way.
comment: Offensive version of Archmage's Seal. Where Archmage's Seal stops everything and freezes the game, Null Burst stops everything and punishes it. If your opponent has 3 spells on the stack this hits for 9. Rewards understanding when your opponent is likely to stack multiple casts.

name: Collapse the Veil
text: Destroy all Wards in play (yours and your opponent's). Deal 3 damage to your opponent for each Ward destroyed this way.
comment: Board wipe for the Ward layer. Destroys everything — including your own — and deals 3 per Ward. Creates a dramatic reset moment late in the game and rewards whoever had more Wards built up. The self-destruction cost prevents it from being purely one-sided.

name: Spark
text: Deal 2 damage to your opponent.
comment: Baseline 1V damage. Mirrors Echo Shield as a clean reference point: a 1V spark hits for exactly the amount Echo Shield prevents on a Warded opponent.

name: Firebolt
text: Deal 3 damage to your opponent. Discard 1 random card from your hand.
comment: Premium 1V damage that burns a card to push damage through — captures the school's identity of spending resources faster than recovery.

name: Burning Hands
text: Deal 1 damage. Place 1 Burn marker on your opponent. (At the start of each of your opponent's turns, each Burn marker on them deals 1 damage; then one Burn marker is removed.)
comment: First Burn card establishes the keyword. DoT is Evocation's answer to Abjuration's slow defensive game — it ticks while the opponent is busy building Wards.

name: Searing Word
text: Deal 2 damage. Your opponent discards 1 random component card from their hand.

name: Catalyst
text: Until the end of the round, your damaging spells deal +1 damage.

name: Kindle
text: Place 1 Burn marker on your opponent.

name: Wild Surge
text: Discard 1 card from your hand. Deal 1 damage to your opponent for each component symbol on the discarded card.
comment: Rewards decks that lean into multi-symbol cards. A discarded VSM tri-component deals 3 damage for a single V cast.

name: Volatile Charge
text: Take 1 damage. Deal 3 damage to your opponent.

name: Battery
text: Deal 2 damage. Draw 1 card from your Resource Deck.
comment: The signature Evocation/Divination splash card. Costs the dual to discourage pure Evo decks from running it for free card advantage.

name: Crackle
text: Deal 1 damage to your opponent. Deal 2 additional damage to a target Ward they control.

name: Inferno Lance
text: Deal 4 damage to your opponent.

name: Hex Bolt
text: Deal 3 damage. This spell cannot be the target of Reactions.
comment: Direct counterplay to Phase Shift and other Reaction-based removal — Evocation's tax on Abjuration's reaction-heavy gameplan.

name: Backdraft
text: When your opponent casts a spell, deal 2 damage to them. The spell still resolves.

name: Searing Riposte
text: When a spell or effect prevents or reduces damage you would deal, deal 2 damage to your opponent.
comment: Punishes Echo Shield and Dampen specifically. Recovers tempo when the opponent successfully blunts your offense.

name: Volatile Bolt
text: When your opponent attaches a Material (M) component card to a prepared spell, deal 2 damage to them.
comment: The Divination hate-piece. Reinforces the rock-paper-scissors triangle: Evocation should beat pure Divination strategies.

name: Combust
text: When your opponent plays a Reaction, deal 4 damage to them. The Reaction still resolves.

name: Fireball
text: Deal 5 damage to your opponent.
comment: Iconic spell at iconic cost. Anchors the L2 power curve.

name: Lightning Bolt
text: Deal 4 damage to your opponent. This damage cannot be reduced below 1.
comment: Damage-reduction effects like Dampen can chip Evo damage to nothing — Lightning Bolt guarantees a sting always lands.

name: Inferno
text: Deal 3 damage. Place 2 Burn markers on your opponent.

name: Scorching Ray
text: Deal 2 damage divided as you choose among your opponent and any Wards they control (minimum 1 damage to each chosen target). Up to three targets total.
comment: Multi-target answer to Ward stacking. Lets Evocation chip through a Ritual Ward + Aegis Eternal board without overcommitting on a single spell.

name: Detonate
text: Discard your hand. Deal 2 damage to your opponent for each card discarded this way.
comment: The all-in turn. Pairs naturally with hand-fixing Items or a freshly drawn 5-card opening hand.

name: Wildfire
text: Place 3 Burn markers on your opponent. Until the end of the round, Burn markers on your opponent also trigger at the start of your turns.
comment: Doubles the Burn tick rate for the round. The Burn-archetype payoff at L2.

name: Channel Pyromancy
text: Until the end of the round, your damaging spells deal +2 damage. You take 1 damage at the start of each of your turns this round.
comment: Self-damage to power up — the school's design pillar in card form.

name: Wrath of the Mage
text: Deal 4 damage. Draw 1 card.
comment: L2 V/M splash. Evo/Div decks get sticky tempo here.

name: Ember Storm
text: Deal 1 damage to your opponent for each component card attached to their prepared spells.
comment: Punishes the opponent for stockpiling — strong against players setting up a big Ritual Ward or Sanctum.

name: Concussive Blast
text: Deal 3 damage. Until the end of the round, your opponent's Reaction spells cost 1 additional component to play.
comment: Anti-Abjuration tax card. A successful cast makes every subsequent Reaction one component more painful.

name: Maelstrom
text: Deal 2 damage. Deal 1 additional damage for each Burn marker on your opponent.
comment: Burn-archetype finisher at L2. Wildfire + Maelstrom is the natural combo line.

name: Searing Backlash
text: When your opponent casts a spell, deal damage to them equal to the number of components attached to that spell. The spell still resolves.
comment: Scales with the opponent's biggest plays. A VVV cast invites a 3-damage backlash; a VVM cast the same.

name: Mana Burn
text: Cancel target spell that requires a Material (M) component. Deal 2 damage to your opponent.
comment: The Evocation parallel to Counterbind — same anti-school role but with damage tacked on rather than pure denial.

name: Flame Riposte
text: When a spell deals damage to you, deal 3 damage to your opponent.

name: Combustive Counter
text: When your opponent plays a Reaction, deal 3 damage to them. The Reaction still resolves.
comment: L2 upgrade of Combust. Stacking both means Reactions become extremely costly for an Abjuration opponent.

name: Meteor
text: Deal 7 damage to your opponent.
comment: L3 baseline raw damage benchmark.

name: Chain Lightning
text: Deal 4 damage to your opponent. Then deal 2 damage to each Ward they control.
comment: Sweeps a Ward-heavy board while still pressuring HP. The hard answer to a stacked Abjuration setup.

name: Pyroclasm
text: Discard your entire hand. Deal 2 damage to your opponent for each card discarded this way.
comment: L3 version of Detonate. With a full hand this is 8-10 damage — the kind of all-in turn the doc describes as the school's core fantasy.

name: Unstoppable Bolt
text: Deal 5 damage. This spell cannot be cancelled, redirected, or reduced.
comment: Hard counter to Abjuration's denial suite. Once cast it WILL resolve — the opponent's only recourse is Wards.

name: Conflagration
text: Place 4 Burn markers on your opponent. Until the end of the round, each Burn marker on them deals an additional 1 damage when it triggers.
comment: Top-end Burn payoff. With Wildfire already in play this turn dials damage to absurd levels — the Burn-archetype's haymaker.

name: Elemental Wrath
text: Deal 3 damage. Deal an additional 2 damage for each Reaction your opponent has cast this round.
comment: Punishes Abjuration directly. The longer they protected themselves the harder this hits.

name: Cataclysm
text: Deal 4 damage. Draw 2 cards.
comment: L3 V/M premium. Evo/Div decks chain Cataclysm into a follow-up cast next turn.

name: Voltaic Overload
text: Take 3 damage. Deal 8 damage to your opponent.
comment: Net 5 damage with self-harm built in. The riskiest L3 — best when you're ahead on the race or out of safer options.

name: Sunburst
text: Destroy all Wards your opponent controls. Deal damage to your opponent equal to the total HP of Wards destroyed this way.
comment: The Ward-Collapse-mirrored spell. Inverts Abjuration's own win condition: instead of converting your Wards to damage you convert THEIRS.

name: Combustive Sigil
text: Until the end of the round, each time your opponent plays a Reaction, deal 3 damage to them.
comment: Lock-out card for the Abjuration matchup. Once this is on the table the opponent must decide whether defending is even worth it.

name: Annihilation Strike
text: When your opponent casts a spell, deal damage equal to twice that spell's level. The spell still resolves.
comment: Scales 2/4/6/8 against L1/L2/L3/L4 spells. Designed to make casting big spells against you feel awful.

name: Final Riposte
text: When a spell deals damage to you, deal that damage doubled to your opponent.

name: Cinder Storm
text: When your opponent casts a spell or Reaction, deal 6 damage to them. The opponent's card still resolves.

name: Apocalypse
text: Deal 12 damage to your opponent. This spell cannot be cancelled, prevented, redirected, or reduced.
comment: The signature finisher. Mirrors Absolute Defense in scope but inverted: where Absolute Defense ends a round in stalemate Apocalypse just ends the game.

name: Phoenix Ascendant
text: Deal 6 damage. Until the end of the round, your damaging spells deal +2 damage and your Burn markers deal double damage.
comment: L4 setup card for an Evo/Div deck that wants to chain spells in the same round. Best when there are still spell slots left to capitalize on.

name: Pyromancer's Reckoning
text: When you take damage from an opponent's spell, deal that damage tripled back to your opponent.
comment: The school's lone L4 Reaction. A 5-damage Fireball into Reckoning is 15 damage back — the threat of this card sitting face-down is itself the deterrent.

name: Insight
text: Draw 2 cards from your Resource Deck.
comment: The school's baseline cantrip and clean reference point: 1 M for 1 card. The consistency engine every Divination deck is built around.

name: Foresight
text: Look at the top 3 cards of your Resource Deck. Put one into your hand and the rest back on top in any order.
comment: Selection over raw draw. Smooths your next two turns and stacks the top of your deck for Premonition or Calculated Draw.

name: Divine
text: Look at the top 2 cards of your Resource Deck. Put one into your hand and the other on the bottom.
comment: Cheap filtering. Trades a little card economy for the guarantee that you keep the piece you actually need.

name: Augury
text: Reveal the top card of your Resource Deck. If it is a Material (M) component, draw it. Otherwise, put it on the bottom.
comment: Rewards a heavily tuned M deck — the more Material you run, the more often this draws. Quietly punishes greedy multi-school splashes.

name: Premonition
text: Draw 2 cards. If either is a multi-symbol component card, draw a third.
comment: Pays off the dual- and tri-component cards Divination leans on to hit off-color costs. A tuned deck turns this into a two-for-one.

name: Recover
text: Return target component card from your discard pile to your hand.
comment: The signature Divination recursion piece named in the design doc. Recycles spent components so the deck effectively never runs dry — the core of out-resourcing a slow Abjuration opponent.

name: Refocus
text: Return a face-up component attached to one of your prepared spells to your hand.
comment: Pick a component back up to re-aim it at a different spell. Pure tempo — undoes a misread or frees a piece for a better line.

name: Scry Glyph
text: Look at the top card of each player's Resource Deck. You may put either one on the bottom of its owner's deck.
comment: Information plus light disruption. Seeing — and pruning — the opponent's next draw is quietly powerful in a long grind.

name: Attune
text: Until the end of the round, the next component you attach to a prepared spell counts as having one additional symbol of a type you choose.
comment: The precision fixer: bridges an off-color cost when you're a single symbol short. Embodies the doc's 'right piece at the right time.'

name: Mind's Eye
text: Draw 1 card, then put one card from your hand on top of your Resource Deck.
comment: Loots without losing cards. Stashes a situational piece for later while digging toward what you need now.

name: Foretell
text: Look at your opponent's hand. Deal 2 damage.
comment: Cheap information. Against a reactive Abjuration deck, knowing which counters they hold lets you sequence safely around them.

name: Omen
text: Prophecy - at the start of your opponent's second turn from now, deal 2 damage to them.
comment: The L1 starter doom - gives the prophecy identity a presence in rounds 1-4 before the L2+ dooms unlock (m12 finding). Restored to 2-in-2 (m3 finding): at 3 the guaranteed clock outpaced Abjuration's entire wall economy.

name: Anticipate
text: When your opponent casts a spell, draw 1 card and deal 1 damage to them.
comment: You saw it coming. A Reaction that stops nothing — it simply converts the opponent's tempo into your card advantage.

name: Reclaim
text: Return up to 2 component cards from your discard pile to your hand.
comment: Scales the recursion plan. Two pieces back from the discard in one cast is how Divination refuels mid-grind.

name: Seek
text: Search your Resource Deck for a component card, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
comment: Consistency incarnate — fetch the exact symbol or dual you're missing. The tutor that lets Divination always have the right piece.

name: Foreknowledge
text: Look at your opponent's hand, then draw 1 card.
comment: Information with a card attached — the value-positive upgrade of Foretell. Reads their plan and pays you for looking.

name: Alchemy
text: Discard any number of cards, then draw that many cards.
comment: Mass filtering. Trade a fistful of off-color bricks for fresh cards without losing card economy.

name: Unbind
text: Destroy target Ward or ongoing effect. Draw 1 card.
comment: A big reason Divination beats Abjuration: it dismantles the Ward shell for free and replaces the card it spent. Nearly dead against Evocation, which runs no Wards.

name: Foreclosure
text: Prophecy - at the start of your opponent's second turn from now, deal 4 damage to them.
comment: The prophecy workhorse - a 4-damage doom on a 2-turn fuse. Announced pressure the opponent must plan around.

name: Quick Study
text: Draw 2 cards, then put one card from your hand on top of your Resource Deck.
comment: Net card advantage with a setup rider. Bank a situational piece on top for next turn while pulling ahead now.

name: Index
text: Look at the top 5 cards of your Resource Deck and put them back in any order.
comment: Stacks your next several turns perfectly. Unflashy, but in a grind it is how Divination guarantees it never bricks.

name: Far Sight
text: Look at the top 3 cards of your Resource Deck and put them back in any order. Prophecy - at the start of your opponent's next turn, deal 2 damage to them.
comment: Scry and doom in one - sculpts your next draws while a short-fuse prophecy lands next turn.

name: Counter-Plan
text: When your opponent casts a spell, return target component attached to it to their hand. If the spell no longer meets its cost, cancel it.
comment: Divination's answer to the stack: not a wall, but a scalpel. Pull one symbol and a greedy, exactly-met spell fizzles itself.

name: Read the Signs
text: When your opponent casts a spell, look at their hand and draw 1 card.
comment: The reactive value engine. Every cast they make tells you their hand and hands you a card.

name: Misdirection
text: Change the target of target spell to another legal target of your choice.
comment: Off-color MS splash signaling Abjuration tech. Send the opponent's removal or burn into a Ward — or somewhere harmless. The classic Divination gotcha.

name: Borrowed Spell
text: Choose a Level 1 spell in your discard pile. Cast a copy of it this turn without paying its cost.
comment: The Evo/Div splash payoff — recast a spent Level 1 spell from your own discard for free. Patches Divination's thin damage with borrowed offense.

name: Echoes of the Past
text: Shuffle your discard pile into your Resource Deck, then draw 2 cards.
comment: A free reshuffle - resets your deck WITHOUT the exhaustion damage a forced recycle costs, then draws 2. Anti-exhaustion tech.

name: Calculated Draw
text: Search your Resource Deck for any card and put it on top, then draw 3 cards.
comment: The payoff tutor: arrange the perfect topdeck, then bury yourself in cards. Card advantage on this scale is how Divination overpowers a defensive opponent.

name: Manipulate Fate
text: Look at the top 5 cards of your Resource Deck, take 2 into your hand, and put the rest back on top in any order.
comment: Deep selection plus advantage. Loads your hand and stacks the deck behind it in a single cast.

name: Perfect Information
text: Look at your opponent's hand and the top 3 cards of their Resource Deck, then draw 2 cards.
comment: The information bomb. Total read on their next several turns while you pull two cards ahead — the precision school at full power.

name: Entropy
text: Prophecy - at the start of your opponent's third turn from now, deal 7 damage to them.
comment: The mid-game doom - 7 damage on a 3-turn fuse; cast early and every one of their turns becomes a countdown.

name: Premeditate
text: Search your Resource Deck for up to 2 component cards, reveal them, add them to your hand, then shuffle.
comment: Double tutor — assemble both halves of an off-color cost at once. The setup that makes a Level 4 turn a certainty rather than a hope.

name: Convergent Future
text: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
comment: Catch-up card advantage. The further behind on cards you are, the bigger it hits — swings a topdeck war hard in your favor.

name: Spellbind
text: When your opponent casts a spell, place a Seal counter on it. It does not resolve and returns to their prepared spells face-down; it cannot be cast again this round.
comment: Borrows Abjuration's Seal counter to lock a threat down for the round. Keeps their components attached but denies the cast — pure tempo theft.

name: Rewind
text: Return target spell that is being cast to its owner's prepared spells face-down. Return all components attached to it to their owner's hand.
comment: Not a counter — a do-over you force on the opponent. They keep the cards but lose the turn and the surprise. Divination's heaviest piece of stack interaction.

name: Borrowed Power
text: Choose a Level 1 or 2 spell in any discard pile. Cast a copy of it this turn without paying its cost.
comment: Off-color MMV. Recur any spent spell — yours or theirs. The Evo/Div splash that finally gives a manipulation deck a real kill turn.

name: Foretold Strike
text: Draw 2 cards. Deal 1 damage to your opponent for each card in your hand above five.
comment: Divination's one concession to a clock: it only rewards the overflowing hand the deck naturally builds, and it costs off-color V. Still far too slow to race Evocation.

name: Mind Theft
text: Look at your opponent's hand and choose a card. They discard it.
comment: Off-color MMS hand attack. Rips the specific counter or bomb you spotted with Foretell out of their plan before they can use it.

name: Omniscience
text: Draw 4 cards. Your spells cannot be countered or redirected this turn.
comment: The archmage's card-advantage engine. Draws into a kill and slams the door on the opponent's Reactions in the same breath.

name: Eternal Return
text: Return all component cards from your discard pile to your hand.
comment: Empties the discard back into your hand. After a long grind this is a dozen-plus cards at once — the ultimate refuel and the recursion school's signature.

name: Grand Design
text: Search your Resource Deck for up to 3 cards, reveal them, add them to your hand, then shuffle.
comment: Assemble any combination you want in one cast. Total control over what comes next — the aspirational Divination payoff.

name: Oblivion
text: Prophecy - at the start of your opponent's third turn from now, deal 9 damage to them. This damage cannot be prevented or absorbed by Wards.
comment: The prophecy haymaker - 9 unwardable damage in 3 turns. Answer it at cast time or race it.

name: Time Spiral
text: Each player shuffles their hand and discard pile into their Resource Deck, then draws 5 cards.
comment: Symmetric on paper, lopsided in practice — you fire it when your deck is thin and your plan is set, resetting the grind entirely on your terms.

name: Convergence
text: Cast a copy of target spell in any discard pile this turn without paying its cost. You may cast one additional spell this turn.
comment: Off-color MMMV ultimate. Recur the biggest spell either player has cast and chain it into another — the Evo/Div deck's explosive finish.

name: Scrying Lens
text: Look at the top 2 cards of your Resource Deck. Put one into your hand and the other back on top.
comment: Card-neutral selection — no advantage, so it's safe at unlimited. Smooths every draw without accelerating you.

name: Component Pouch
text: Reveal the top card of your Resource Deck. If it is a component card, put it into your hand; otherwise, put it on the bottom.
comment: Digs only for components and whiffs in trainer-heavy hands, which self-limits how many you'd run.

name: Cantrip Scroll
text: Draw a card, then put a card from your hand on top of your Resource Deck.
comment: Card-neutral loot that banks a situational card for next turn. Pure smoothing, no card gain.

name: Spare Reagents
text: Discard a card, then draw a card.
comment: Turns a brick into a fresh card with no net advantage — fair to run in any quantity.

name: Transmuter's Stone
text: Choose a basic (single-symbol) component card in your hand. Until end of turn, treat it as a different basic component of your choice.
comment: Fixes color but not count, so it helps splashes hit off-color symbols without trivially building doubles. Only reshapes a card you already hold.

name: Mnemonic Charm
text: Return target component card from your discard pile to the top of your Resource Deck.
comment: Deliberately slower than Divination's Recover (top of deck, not hand) so it complements that school instead of replacing it.

name: Empowered Chalk
text: The next damaging spell you cast this turn deals +1 damage.
comment: Each one is a card spent, so stacking takes real investment; +1 is small enough to leave unlimited.

name: Bulwark Shard
text: A Ward you control gains 2 HP.
comment: Free Ward sustain, but dead with no Ward in play — strictly a Ward-deck include.

name: Arcane Study
text: Draw 2 cards, then put a card from your hand on top of your Resource Deck.
comment: Net +1 with a bank — the workhorse draw. The one-Gambit-per-turn cap is the main brake on free card advantage.

name: Forbidden Tome
text: Discard your hand, then draw 4 cards.
comment: Costs your current hand, so it's a real decision rather than free value. Best when flooded or empty.

name: Mentor's Guidance
text: Discard a card, then search your Resource Deck for any one card, reveal it, add it to your hand, and shuffle.
comment: Universal tutor; the discard cost plus one-per-turn keep it honest. The enabler that lets splash decks find their off-color piece.

name: Recharge
text: Search your Resource Deck for a same-symbol dual component (VV, SS, or MM), reveal it, add it to your hand, and shuffle.
comment: Tutors the doubles that pay for L3/L4 costs — the clean way to make high-level ramp consistent without touching the 2-card cap.

name: Salvage
text: Return target component card from your discard pile to your hand.
comment: One component back, once per turn — slower than Divination's spell recursion, so it supports the plan rather than owning it.

name: Premonition Charm
text: Look at the top 4 cards of your Resource Deck and put them back on top in any order.
comment: Deep deck sculpting with no card gain. One-per-turn keeps it below Divination's Index.

name: Ritual Circle
text: Reveal the top card of your Resource Deck. If it is a component card, attach it to one of your prepared spells; otherwise, put it into your hand.
comment: A bonus attachment beyond your one-per-turn limit, but off the top of the deck — ramp with built-in variance.

name: Overclock
text: Take 2 damage. This turn, you may cast 1 additional spell, provided you have a free spell slot and its components are already attached.
comment: Extra cast is the biggest tempo swing here — gated by needing a second spell pre-assembled and a free slot. The highest-variance trainer; playtest before printing many.

name: Second Wind
text: Gain 5 HP.
comment: The anti-aggro stabilizer. One-per-turn stops burst healing, and it's dead against control, so it's a tech include, not a staple.

name: Battle Trance
text: Lose 2 HP. The next spell you cast this turn deals +3 damage.
comment: Risk-for-reward, on theme for the burn school. Self-damage plus one-per-turn keep the lethal-turn push in check.

name: Aegis Charm
text: Create a Ward with 3 HP.
comment: Gives any school a taste of defense, but deliberately vanilla — it can't match Abjuration's Ward engine of buffs, recursion, and damage conversion.

name: Dispelling Powder
text: Destroy target Ward or ongoing effect.
comment: The universal answer to the Ward shell; completely dead against Evocation, so running it is a matchup choice.

name: Quenching Salts
text: Remove all Burn markers from yourself, then gain 1 HP for each marker removed this way.
comment: Pure anti-Burn tech, useless outside that matchup. The lifegain rider rewards letting Burn stack before you clear it.

name: Resolve
text: Until the start of your next turn, your spells cannot be canceled or redirected by your opponent.
comment: Pushes a key spell through a wall of counters — the aggro and combo answer to a control deck.

name: Iron Will
text: Until the start of your next turn, your opponent cannot make you discard cards or remove cards from your hand or prepared spells.
comment: Shields a planned turn from hand-stripping like Mind Theft or Interrupt. Narrow and proactive.

name: Sealed Vault
text: Shuffle your discard pile into your Resource Deck.
comment: Anti-exhaustion tech - a free recycle that dodges the escalating reshuffle damage. Dead against a fast clock.

name: Mana Sickness
text: Until the start of your next turn, your opponent cannot draw cards except their normal draw at the start of their turn.
comment: Shuts off the card-advantage turn. Useless against aggro, premium against a draw-heavy deck.

name: Spite
text: Until the start of your next turn, each time your opponent plays a Reaction spell, they lose 2 HP.
comment: A free, splashable version of Evocation's reaction-punishers; dead against a deck that isn't holding up Reactions.

name: Saboteur's Kit
text: Prophecy - at the start of your opponent's second turn from now, deal 2 damage to them.
comment: A free time-bomb for any deck - 2 damage on a 2-turn fuse. Modest alone; stacked copies build a schedule of dooms.

name: Disarm
text: Look at your opponent's hand. You may choose a component card in it; that card's owner puts it on top of their Resource Deck.
comment: Soft disruption: bounce a key piece for a tempo hit (they redraw it, so it's not a two-for-one) and read their hand.
